note: SAMPLE DATA — all events below are fictional placeholders. This file is overwritten by scripts/fetch_feeds.py once GitHub Actions is enabled.

events.summary: Sample event: shipping industry advisory notes elevated transit risk in the southern Red Sea corridor.
events.why_it_matters: Chokepoint disruptions raise insurance and rerouting costs and can pull naval escorts into the region.

events.summary: Sample event: publicly announced military exercise reported near the median line, above seasonal baseline.
events.why_it_matters: Exercise tempo above baseline is a classic open-source indicator; the signal is the deviation from normal.

events.summary: Sample event: public reporting of disruption at a grain-export terminal affecting corridor throughput.
events.why_it_matters: Black Sea export capacity links the war directly to global food prices.

events.summary: Sample event: national CERT publishes advisory on intrusion activity affecting utility-sector vendors.
events.why_it_matters: Vendor-level compromises are supply-chain risk: one intrusion cascades to many downstream operators.

events.summary: Sample event: public tanker-tracking data shows a week-over-week dip in Hormuz transits.
events.why_it_matters: Roughly a fifth of global oil transits Hormuz; verified flow changes move markets.

events.summary: Sample event: publicly announced carrier strike group deployment with stated theater assignment.
events.why_it_matters: Publicly announced posture changes are deliberate signals; tracking them is core open-source order-of-battle work.

events.summary: Sample event: reported close-quarters interaction between coast guard vessels near a disputed feature.
events.why_it_matters: Gray-zone friction below the conflict threshold is where miscalculation risk lives.

events.summary: Sample event: public reporting of strikes affecting power-generation capacity ahead of peak demand season.
events.why_it_matters: Energy infrastructure is a recurring campaign target; capacity trends support humanitarian and economic forecasting.

events.summary: Sample event: public reporting of cross-border fire; details unconfirmed by second source at time of entry.
events.why_it_matters: Single-source kinetic reports are where confidence labeling earns its keep — logged, flagged, watched for corroboration.

events.summary: Sample event: reported expansion of export-control rules covering advanced chipmaking tools.
events.why_it_matters: Export controls are the economic front of great-power competition; they shift capability trajectories more than most kinetic events.

events.summary: Sample event: advisory warns of credential-phishing campaign targeting defense-sector suppliers.
events.why_it_matters: The defense industrial base is a persistent espionage target; supplier-tier security is a known weak link.
